label: End time [s]
help: Simulation end time in seconds. The engine uses a fine grid (0.01 s steps) near t=0 and 1 s steps thereafter, so runtime scales roughly linearly.

label: Rated power [MW]
help: Rated thermal power of the reactor [MW]. Used to normalise decay heat, set the initial heat flux for core temperatures, and scale rod-failure counts.

label: Radial peaking F_r
help: Radial hot-channel factor: ratio of peak-to-average pin power across the core. Multiplied by F_z to give the combined hot-pin heat flux used in DNBR and PCT.

label: Axial peaking F_z
help: Axial peaking factor: ratio of peak-to-average axial heat flux (chopped cosine). Typical fresh-core value ~1.55. Combined with F_r for hot-pin calculations.

label: Gap conductance [W/m²K]
help: Fuel-clad gap conductance. Controls the temperature drop across the as-fabricated He-filled gap. Lower values raise fuel centreline temperature; typical BOL range 3000–9000 W/m²K.

label: Initial pressure [kPa]
help: Initial RCS pressure at t=0 [kPa]. Standard PWR full-power operating pressure ~15 500 kPa (2250 psia).

label: Core-exit temp [°C]
help: Initial bulk coolant temperature at the core exit [°C]. Sets the initial enthalpy and the starting point for moderator reactivity feedback in RIA cases.

label: Doppler coeff [pcm/°C]
help: Doppler (fuel temperature) reactivity coefficient [pcm/°C]. Negative value provides prompt negative feedback during power excursions. Typical PWR value: −2 to −4 pcm/°C.

label: MTC [pcm/°C]
help: Moderator temperature coefficient [pcm/°C]. Must be negative at power for inherent stability. Typical PWR full-power value: −20 to −40 pcm/°C.

label: Trip delay [s]
help: Signal processing and relay delay applied to every reactor protection system trip. The scram fires this many seconds after the setpoint is first exceeded. Typical hard-wired PWR RPS: 1.5 s.

label: High-power trip frac
help: High-flux scram setpoint as a fraction of rated power (e.g. 1.10 = trip at 110%). Applies to both the RPS evaluation and the point-kinetics high-power check in RIA cases.

label: Low-P trip [kPa]
help: Low-pressure scram setpoint [kPa]. The reactor trips when RCS pressure falls below this value. Set to 0 to disable. Default is 90% of the initial RCS pressure.

_note: ─── FLARECON containment parameters (only shown when FLARECON sheet exists) ───

label: Condensation model multiplier
help: Multiplier on the wall condensation heat flux — applies to whichever model is selected (Uchida or DLM-FM). Range 0.5–1.5 per GRS/AREVA study. Values < 1 are conservative (reduce heat removal, raise peak pressure).

label: Concrete conductivity [W/m·K]
help: Thermal conductivity of the concrete biological shield. Controls long-term heat absorption and pressure decay rate. Typical range 1.0–2.5 W/(m·K). Lower values are conservative (slower pressure decay).

label: Liner–concrete gap [m]
help: Air gap thickness between the steel liner and concrete wall. Largest single uncertainty in the liner-to-concrete thermal path. Range 0–3 mm. Larger gaps reduce heat conduction and raise peak pressure.

label: PCC/outer cooling fraction
help: Fraction of wall area with active outer-surface cooling. Set to 0 for conventional dry containments with no credited outer cooling. For AP1000-type PCC, typical range 0.3–1.0.

label: Outer wall temperature [°C]
help: Outer surface temperature boundary condition, active when pcc_fraction > 0. For mPower PCCT: nominal 40.6°C (105°F), range ~20–65°C per MPWR-TECR-005062.
